[PDF_PAGE 76 | score=21] - Time Stop I against destruction, as above). It moves inland at a rate of 50 yards per round, the damage dimin- ishes by Id6 each round until it ceases to exist 500 yards inland. This spell is so exhausting that after casting this spell the caster is unable to cast magical spells for ld4 days. The material component of this spell is a wden plank, struck forcibly acmss the surface of the water in the direction the tidal wave is to form. Notes: Uncommon in the Sea of Fallen Stars in the FORGOTTEN RBALMS setting and for Sea mages; otherwise, very rare. [PDF_PAGE 62 | score=17] > Temporal Shell Notes: Restricted to chronomancers; common. Temporal Freedom (Alteration) Level: 8 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 rd. Duration: 1 day Area of Effect: Creature touched Saving Throw: None This spell provides protection against any spell creating a stasis effect that would affect the protected creature. Examples of such spells in- clude Phezultk sleep ofages. temporal stasis, and time stop. For example. a being within the con- iines of a time stop and protected by temporal freedom remains free to hear, see, and act; but could pretend to he frozen until the caster of the time stop drew near, turned away, or lei? himself open to attack. After providing its prote
[PDF_PAGE 203 | score=16] - Advanced Domination at least eight hours per day in isolation. Once the enchantment is cast, the wizard shapes the area of effect. This totals ten 100 yard by 100 yard squares, plus five additional squares per level of the caster above 20th (a 25th-level wizard could, for example, lay them end to end in a barrier 100 yards wide and 3,500 yards, or just over two miles, long. The area of effect can be shaped as desired. The spell does not affect any vegetation in the area. Any creature putting its full weight on the af- fected area suffers damage. The first round of contact inflicts 1 point of damage; the second round, one point of damage per Hit Die; on the third and subsequent rounds Id3 p
[PDF_PAGE 77 | score=14] Timed Stasis f Note: It is recommended that the DM use a stop watch or silently count to time this spell. If the caster is unable to complete the intended action before the spell duration expires, he will probably be caught in an embarrassing situation. The use of a ieleport spell before the expiration of the time stop spell is permissible. Notes: Common spell (PHB). Timed Stasis (Alteration) (Chronomancy) Level: 7 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 turn Duration: ld3+1 months Area of Effect: 1 object, no more than 27 cu. ft. Saving Throw: None This potent dweomer renders a single object completely impervious to all physical, magical, or scrying effects for a preset period. For
[PDF_PAGE 120 | score=14] > Volley damage each round. For example, an 8th-level wizard inflicts 8d4 damage with this spell on the first round, 6d4 on the second round, 4d4 on the third round, 2d4 on the fourth round, and the spell ends in the fifth round. Each round, the subject is allows to a saving throw-the spell ends when the creature succeeds, or when the acid damage runs its course. The acid can also be neutralized with soda, ash, lye, charcoal, or removed with a large quantity of water. The vitriolic sphere also splashes acid in a 5- foot radius around the primary target. Any crea- tures within the splash radius must make a successiid saving throw vs. paralyzation or suffer a splash hit that inflicts ld4 point
[PDF_PAGE 148 | score=14] \ Whip pare. The magnitude of the shift depends on the type of attack. If the attack inflicts physical damage, half the damage is applied immediately, and the other half is put off for the duration of the spell. This delays the need for healing, but chronomancers are cau- tioned not to lose track of the time since casting. After the first turn, the spell could expire at any moment, and all deferred damage is then applied at once. This could easily kill the caster. Healing potions and spells are applied first to received damage, then to deferred damage. If the attack produces a spell effect (imprison- ment and energy drain inflict no physical damage but require a touch), then the spell's effe
[PDF_PAGE 204 | score=14] - Defiler Metvnorghosis I a t t 1 I c C a i t a C I successful system shock or die in the mutation )recess. Any divergence from the letter of the ;pel1 description or interruption of the casting irwess kills the caster immediately. The exact components, preparation time, and :asting times differ, depending on the level the lefiler is about to achieve, grouped by level into bwt middle, high, andfinalmetamorphosis. In all :ases, living creatures used in the casting must be within one mile of the caster. All castings of this pell, except that for the first at Zlst level, require he caster's dragon orb as a focus as well. Low Metamorphosis (21st-23rd levels): The reparation time for each of thes
[PDF_PAGE 78 | score=12] ately reapplied. Any resources used to heal the target in the intervening time are entirely wasted. The standard chances of failure still apply. This spell places extreme temporal stresses on the caster and the timestream, as well as attract- ing unwanted attention. It can be safely cast no more than once per day. Notes: Restricted to chronomancers; common. Timereaver Lost spellfmm the 3rd Age of mn.
